growth_records.strain: P3-Ar5 (engineered, plasmid-free; derived from P. chlororaphis P3 / HT66)

yields.product_yields: arbutin up to 6.79 g/L titer (54-fold over initial strain)

growth_records.cite: Wang, Songwei et al. Enhanced biosynthesis of arbutin by engineering shikimate pathway in Pseudomonas chlororaphis P3. Microbial Cell Factories (2018). DOI:10.1186/s12934-018-1022-8. [PMC6230248]
growth_records.snippet: Specific growth rate (h-1) 0.133 +/- 0.002 | 0.132 +/- 0.002 | 0.133 +/- 0.003 | 0.131 +/- 0.002 | 0.132 +/- 0.001 ... After 24-72 h culturing at 28 C and 200 rpm. KBG (Glycerol 15 mL, Soy peptone 20 g/L, Glucose 9 g/L, 0.4% Tween-80, MgSO4
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (Pseudomonas chlororaphis P3-Ar5). Specific growth rate reported directly (Table 'Growth kinetic parameters of P3-Ar5 feeding different concentration of arbutin'): 0.133,0.132,0.133,0.131,0.132 h-1 at 2,3,4,5,6 g/L added arbutin; mean ~0.132 h-1. Doubling time td=ln2/0.132=5.25 h. Temperature 28C and 200 rpm and pH 6.8 (restored to 6.8) stated. Biomass conversion factor given: 1 OD600 = 0.4135 g DCW/L. Arbutin productivity 0.094 g/L/h is the final best fed-batch value (also reported as titer 6.79 g/L). No per-gDW uptake reported for this specific assay.

growth_records.medium: KB/KBG-based fermentation medium with glucose + 4-hydroxybenzoate (4-HBA) mixed fed-batch

yields.product_yields: final arbutin 6.79 g/L; glucose is preferred C source, glycerol used after glucose depletion

growth_records.cite: Wang, Songwei et al. Enhanced biosynthesis of arbutin by engineering shikimate pathway in Pseudomonas chlororaphis P3. Microbial Cell Factories (2018). DOI:10.1186/s12934-018-1022-8. [PMC6230248]
growth_records.snippet: When feeding 4-HBA and glucose at 24 h, after 12 h culturing most of the glucose and 4-HBA were consumed with 5.69 g/L arbutin production at 36 h with the productivity of 0.16 g/L/h, and the glucose uptake rate was recorded to be 383.3 mg/L
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote, fed-batch condition. Measured volumetric glucose uptake rates reported directly: 383.3 mg/L/h (after 24 h feed) and 410.9 mg/L/h (after 36 h feed). mmol conversion: 383.3/180.16=2.13 mmol/L/h; 410.9/180.16=2.28 mmol/L/h (glucose MW 180.16). Cannot convert to per-gDW since biomass concentration at those timepoints not tabulated. Arbutin (product) productivities reported at 36/48/60 h; overall best 0.094 g/L/h, titer 6.79 g/L. Temperature 28C, 200 rpm, pH ~6.8. NOTE: an earlier baseline (strain P3-Ar1) on glycerol-only KB medium gave arbutin yield 0.007 g/g glycerol and productivity 0.0018 g/L/h; later engineered strains up to 0.12 g/g glycerol.
